mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession probability, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion and construction sector acceleration, driving corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly industrial production growth exceeding 0.5 percent during an economic expansion confirms robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, reinforcing earnings momentum and generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power during mid-cycle expansions, with 5-day forward returns showing no significant positive correlation (Rank IC < 0.01) over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year CPI inflation surpassing 2.5 percent forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, resulting in negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5 percent, or the negative predictive coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding employment and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive long-horizon equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to generate positive 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year out-of-sample window, or the signal reverses direction during normal economic conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling unemployment creates a goldilocks macro environment that maximizes corporate profit margins and equity risk appetite, driving positive medium-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of triggered cases, or interaction term coefficients are insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing demand, triggering earnings downgrades and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during late-cycle expansions fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 45 percent or Rank IC consistently above 0.02.
